rationale: Simonson (2014) is cited for two specific argumentative functions: to explain how preference heterogeneity can wash out aggregate repulsion/attraction effects, and to question the fidelity of some claimed replications. These references are integrated into the citing paper's discussion of moderators and replication standards rather than used as a generic literature-list mention. The citation therefore plays a meaningful interpretive and positioning role, even though the citing paper is not directly extending or disputing Simonson's framework.
relation_description: The citing commentary uses Simonson (2014) as a substantive companion source to support its interpretation of boundary conditions and replication validity in attraction-effect research. It draws on Simonson's heterogeneity and replication-fidelity arguments to reinforce its own critique of overgeneralized null-replication claims.
